raw_consumer_statement: I am filing a complaint against American Express for failing to conduct a reasonable investigation under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ) regarding a disputed charge of {$2200.00} from XXXX XXXX ( Amex XXXX : XXXX and XXXX ). XXXX XXXX involuntarily canceled my connecting flight to my final destination, breaking my continuous itinerary. Under XXXX DOT regulations ( 14 CFR 260 ), this entitled me to a full refund, rendering the itinerary a " Trip in Vain. '' XXXX XXXX falsely told Amex this was a " voluntary '' cancellation to keep my {$2200.00} upgrade fee. When I attempted to appeal Amex 's decision and provide new, irrefutable documentation proving the airline-initiated cancellationand proving I have filed a formal US DOT enforcement complaint against the airlineAmex phone representatives refused to accept my documents, refused to reopen the dispute, and stated the case was permanently closed. Amex failed to properly investigate the merchant 's false claims and is now blocking my legal right to appeal with new evidence. I am requesting that Amex Executive Resolutions reopen this case, accept my documentation, and permanently reverse this {$2200.00} charge
